title: rapuntium grande
intro: Rapuntium grande, also known as Wild Turnip or Rapunzel, is a perennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, native to Europe and western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Rapuntium grande can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots can also be used in salads and as a cooking green.
